items.title: Bayes' theorem factor flow
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  prior["P(A) prior"]
  like["P(B|A) likelihood"]
  joint["P(A ∩ B) = P(B|A)P(A)"]
  evid["P(B) evidence"]
  post["P(A|B) posterior"]
  prior --> joint
  like --> joint
  joint --> post
  evid --> post
items.caption: The posterior P(A|B) combines prior and likelihood, normalized by the evidence P(B).

items.title: Reversing conditioning with Bayes
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  forward["Known: P(B|A)"]
  reverse["Want: P(A|B)"]
  bayes["Bayes: P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B)"]
  total["Compute P(B) via total probability"]
  forward --> bayes
  total --> bayes
  bayes --> reverse
items.caption: Bayes' theorem is the bridge from forward conditional probability P(B|A) to the reverse P(A|B).

items.title: Law of total probability for P(B)
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  a1["A1 with P(A1)"]
  a2["A2 with P(A2)"]
  an["... partition covers sample space"]
  b["Event B"]
  sum["P(B) = Σ P(B|Ai)P(Ai)"]
  a1 -->|"P(B|A1)P(A1)"| sum
  a2 -->|"P(B|A2)P(A2)"| sum
  an -->|"..."| sum
  sum --> b
items.caption: When A₁, A₂, … form a partition, the evidence P(B) is the weighted sum of likelihoods across hypotheses.
